purpose: Fifth AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering the self-mailbox init-command subset that reconfigures a stem cell into processor-left.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular self-mailbox init node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular self-mailbox init node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular self-mailbox init node.

layers.summary: A proc-l-init self-mailbox command reconfigures the stem node into a processor with left memory and clears the mailbox.
layers.boundary: This layer states symbolic self-mailbox init execution only; it does not execute write-buffer, standard-signal, or neighbor-delivery commands.

layers.summary: The self-mailbox init node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one self-mailbox init activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0030 init-command subset and adds no neighbor delivery or full buffer execution.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested self-mailbox init command.
